summary: Deleted the old handwritten/placeholder assembly file `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.s`, removing 2997 lines of HSD light-object assembly from the asm tree. The slice contains no PR body or review discussion and shows only the deletion, so the likely decompilation significance is that the corresponding `lobj.c` implementation had become sufficient to replace the assembly object.

key_files.notes: Removed the assembly file containing HSD_LObj light-object routines.

what_changed: Deleted assembly definitions for HSD light-object routines visible in the diff excerpt, including `HSD_LObjGetFlags`, `HSD_LObjSetFlags`, `HSD_LObjClearFlags`, light-mask getters, active-light accessors, animation helpers, `HSD_LObjGetLightVector`, and `HSD_LObjSetup`.
No C or header changes are present in this PR slice; the diff excerpt only proves the asm file deletion.
smart_moves: Used a clean whole-file asm deletion rather than leaving a partially stale assembly fallback, which makes future build/link ownership clearer.
Retired a large baselib assembly unit at once, reducing the amount of un-decompiled code tracked under `asm/sysdolphin/baselib`.
Kept the PR narrowly scoped to `lobj.s`, making the change easy to review and merge quickly.
decomp_lessons: When a baselib C translation is ready, removing the corresponding `asm/.../*.s` file is the final proof-of-integration step; keep these PRs minimal and focused.
Large asm removals are easiest to audit when the PR only deletes the obsolete assembly file and does not mix in unrelated cleanup.
For future LObj work, preserve externally visible HSD symbol names such as `HSD_LObjGetFlags` and `HSD_LObjSetup` so assembly references can be retired without link churn.
Evidence for the underlying matching strategy is weak in this slice because no replacement C diff, build-map update, PR body, or reviewer comments are included.
naming_conventions: Public HSD light-object APIs use `HSD_LObj...` Pascal-style names, e.g. `HSD_LObjGetFlags`, `HSD_LObjAnimAll`, `HSD_LObjReqAnimAll`.
Internal helper shown in the removed asm uses `LObjUpdateFunc` without the `HSD_` prefix.
Small-data/global names in the assembly excerpt use lower snake case, e.g. `lightmask_diffuse`, `lightmask_attnfunc`, `lightmask_alpha`, `lightmask_specular`, `nb_active_lights`, `active_lights`.
Placeholder/static data labels in the old asm use generated names such as `HSD_LObj_803B94A0` and `HSD_LObj_803B94AC`; future cleanup should replace these only when evidence supports better names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The deleted assembly exposed common LObj matching patterns that replacement C likely had to reproduce: null-object guards returning early or zero, halfword flag operations at offset `0x8`, active-light bounds checks, jump-table style animation update dispatch, and calls into AObj/WObj animation routines.
The old asm used PowerPC bit operations such as `or`, `andc`, `rlwinm`, and `clrlwi` for flag management; matching C should express these as precise bitmask operations.

Animation code in the removed file routed through `HSD_AObjInterpretAnim`, `HSD_WObjInterpretAnim`, `HSD_AObjReqAnim`, and `HSD_WObjReqAnim`, indicating LObj matching depends on AObj/WObj integration.

Lighting setup code in the deleted excerpt called `GXInitLightColor` and vector math helpers like `PSVECSubtract` and `PSVECNormalize`, so replacement C must preserve GX/PPC float/vector call behavior.
Because this PR slice only contains a deletion, exact matching tactics used to make the C compile identically are not directly visible.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the PR context.
The PR merged quickly on 2024-02-01, but there is no textual evidence of reviewer rationale in this slice.
follow_up_queries: Find the PR or commit that introduced or completed the C implementation for `src/sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.c` before this deletion.
Search for build-system or linker map changes around PR 1172 to confirm how `lobj.c` replaced `lobj.s`.
Inspect current `lobj.c` and headers for documented names replacing generated labels such as `HSD_LObj_803B94A0` and `HSD_LObj_803B94AC`.
Compare neighboring baselib asm-removal PRs for the same minimal deletion pattern.
